verse_text: He also spoke this parable: 'A certain man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard, and he came seeking fruit on it and found none.

links.context: The trees bear their fruit: In this verse Joel foreshadows the cross of Christ, the tree which bore the greatest fruit of all. The vine and the fig tree refer to the establishment of the Church (see [[ROM.11:17]]-24; [[JOH.15:1]]; [[LUK.13:6]]-9). Rain, wheat, wine, and oil refer to the food that is right for you, namely the sacraments of Holy Baptism, the Eucharist, and the sealing of the Holy Spirit.

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden

links.context: The fig tree is a figure of Israel, God's people before the Incarnation ([[HOS.9:10]]; Mt 21:19-21; [[MRK.11:13]], [[MRK.11:20]], [[MRK.11:21]]; [[LUK.13:6]], [[LUK.13:7]]). But the figs are young and immature, like those believers who have not grown in their faith ([[1CO.3:1]]). The true vine is Christ ([[JOH.15:1]]-5), and the vines are the Church, the New Israel. We are the fragrance of Christ to God the Father ([[2CO.2:15]]).

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden
